keyPoints: Metacognitive design elements (reflective prompts, confidence judgments, explanations, error-spotting feedback) shaping calibration across domains.
Differentiation between metacognitive calibration and short-term performance gains, with calibration benefits emerging in transfer and long-term use.
Explicit calibration metrics (calibration curves, Brier scores, confidence–accuracy correlations) required in evaluation.
Risks of AI-induced overconfidence and algorithmic self-deception without safeguards (uncertainty signaling, human-in-the-loop checks).
Interventions that couple confidence judgments with evidence-based feedback and direct error analysis, while managing cognitive load.
Prompt personalization to learner profiles (prior performance, cognitive traits, domain expertise) to maximize calibration and minimize burden.
Robust, preregistered, longitudinal, multi-domain study designs with calibration metrics reported alongside performance.
